title: Science + Green Materials: Karnataka Class 8 — Metals and Fuels
subject: Science

questions.text: Karnataka is investing in solar energy to replace coal power plants. A 1 MW coal plant burns 300 tonnes of coal/day emitting 800 tonnes of CO₂. A 1 MW solar farm emits 0 tonnes of CO₂ daily. If Karnataka has 50 such coal plants, how much CO₂ is saved daily by converting all to solar?
questions.explanation: CO₂ saved = 50 × 800 = 40,000 tonnes/day. That's 1.46 crore tonnes/year! Karnataka already generates 14,700 MW of renewable energy. Your combustion and coal chapters explain exactly why India is racing to solar power.
questions.subject: Science
questions.chapter: Coal and Petroleum / Combustion and Flame
